text.title: ruby-rails-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ruby-rails-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Multiple files defining ServiceResult with different method names (ok?, success?, result?, error?).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Controllers must handle every variant. Add a CI step that greps for class ServiceResult and fails if more than one definition exists. Lock to success?/failure? only.

failure-mode.description: Sidekiq jobs that accept ActiveRecord objects directly: perform(user) instead of perform(user.id).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ActiveRecord objects serialise as YAML and load a potentially stale record when the worker runs. Pass IDs; load fresh records inside perform.

failure-mode.description: Sidekiq jobs without idempotency — double-charging, double-sending on retry.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Workers retry on failure. Idempotency requires checking a unique key before performing the side effect. Use a database flag or SidekiqUniqueJobs.

failure-mode.description: N+1 queries in ActiveModel::Serializer or Jbuilder templates, not caught by controller specs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Serialisers run after the controller action. Bullet must be enabled in test mode with raise: true and serialiser specs must be run, not just controller specs.

failure-mode.description: LLMs invent slightly different ServiceResult shapes per file (sometimes result.ok?, sometimes result.success?, sometimes result.failure?). Lock the API in CI.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Controllers must handle every variant. Add a CI step that greps for class ServiceResult and fails if more than one definition exists.

failure-mode.description: Agents put UserMailer.welcome(user).deliver_now between save! and end of transaction — a rolled-back insert leaves a sent email. Always deliver_later after commit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: deliver_now within a transaction runs before commit completes.

failure-mode.description: N+1 patterns reappear in serializers (ActiveModel::Serializer/Jbuilder); reviewer must check serializer specs, not just controller specs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Serializers run after the controller action, outside the spec's SQL spy.

failure-mode.description: Sidekiq jobs generated by AI often accept ActiveRecord objects directly — pass IDs, not objects, to avoid serialization of stale state.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AR objects serialize as YAML and load stale records when the worker runs.

failure-mode.description: Agents skip Pundit::Authorize on new actions; require verify_authorized and verify_policy_scoped in ApplicationController.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pundit does not enforce authorization automatically without these hooks.

failure-mode.description: Generated migrations are sometimes destructive (drop_column without backfill). Require reversible migrations and review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Irreversible migrations cannot be rolled back during deploys.

failure-mode.description: Agents may set config.active_job.queue_adapter = :inline for "tests", masking timing bugs in production where Sidekiq is async.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inline mode hides concurrency issues that emerge with async workers.
